limitations: Simple observed speed derivative from two displacement-derived velocity windows only.
Second derivatives amplify tracking noise; missing endpoints or implausible spikes produce UNKNOWN.
Does not infer effort, sprint quality, physical capacity, intent, role, pressure-breaking quality, or tactical causation.
Acceleration/deceleration PASS means the observed speed change exceeded frozen thresholds, not that a player deliberately burst or braked.

definition.description: Anchor-record field containing the tracked player id.
definition.name: entity_id_field

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field where acceleration is evaluated.
definition.name: frame_field

definition.description: Length of each non-overlapping displacement-velocity window.
definition.name: lookback_seconds

definition.description: Frozen plausibility limit used to mark likely tracking-noise spikes UNKNOWN.
definition.name: maximum_abs_acceleration_mps2

definition.description: Frozen plausibility limit for velocity-window speeds.
definition.name: maximum_player_speed_mps

definition.description: Minimum absolute acceleration magnitude required for PASS.
definition.name: minimum_abs_acceleration_mps2

definition.description: Minimum absolute speed change required for acceleration/deceleration PASS.
definition.name: minimum_abs_delta_speed_mps

limitations: v0.1 supports adjacent same-team action pairs only.
Does not infer tactical intent, planned combination, or causation.

definition.description: Number of ordered actions in the chain. v0.1 supports adjacent pairs.
definition.name: chain_length

definition.description: Maximum elapsed provider event time between adjacent actions.
definition.name: maximum_action_gap_seconds

limitations: Uses provider events for action identity and nearest tracking frame for alignment.
Does not prove controlled reception, intent, optimality, or tactical role.

definition.description: Provider event action type to expose as anchors.
definition.name: action_type

limitations: Depends on canonical pitch coordinates and known pitch width.
Claims only observed same-player movement with the ball under a conservative control criterion.
The base primitive is direction-neutral; progressive carry is a composition over forward_progression_m.
Does not infer dribbling skill, pressure-breaking quality, player intent, tactical causation, decision quality, or optimality.
Does not claim the carry beat, bypassed, or eliminated a defender.
Loose-ball, ambiguous-control, possession-change, or missing-tracking intervals are FAIL or UNKNOWN rather than carried across.

definition.description: Maximum ball-carrier distance used by the control criterion.
definition.name: control_distance_m

definition.description: Touch-tolerant maximum velocity-vector difference between ball and carrier for co-moving evidence.
definition.name: maximum_ball_player_speed_delta_mps

definition.description: Maximum interval from controlled reception to the carrier's next confirmed pass release.
definition.name: maximum_carry_seconds

definition.description: Maximum missing ball/player tracking ratio before UNKNOWN.
definition.name: maximum_missing_frame_ratio

definition.description: Minimum velocity-observed-frame ratio satisfying touch-tolerant co-moving evidence.
definition.name: minimum_comoving_frame_ratio

definition.description: Minimum observed-frame ratio satisfying the distance/nearest-player control criterion.
definition.name: minimum_controlled_frame_ratio

definition.description: Minimum carrier displacement required before PASS; distinguishes carries from stationary holds.
definition.name: minimum_displacement_m

definition.description: Margin allowing shared teammate proximity while preserving the controlled-pass control model.
definition.name: nearest_teammate_margin_m

limitations: Compares declared numeric evidence values only.
Does not infer why the change happened or whether it was tactically caused by the anchor event.

definition.description: After-record status field that must be PASS, or none to compare the numeric value without requiring after status.
definition.name: after_status_field

definition.description: After-record numeric evidence field to compare.
definition.name: after_value_field

definition.description: Before-record status field that must be PASS.
definition.name: before_status_field

definition.description: Before-record numeric evidence field to compare.
definition.name: before_value_field

definition.description: Direction of the required numeric change.
definition.name: change_mode

definition.description: Maximum allowed before value for compactness-style checks.
definition.name: maximum_before_value_m

definition.description: Minimum required change in metres.
definition.name: minimum_change_m

definition.description: Required before/after status value for numeric change evaluation.
definition.name: required_status_value

limitations: Requires a supplied observed line and exact anchor-correlated release/reception relative-position evidence.
Does not identify whether the supplied line is a first, second, midfield, or back line.
Does not infer tactical intention, pass optimality, causation, or defensive intent.

definition.description: Distance from the supplied line treated as level rather than ahead or behind.
definition.name: line_buffer_m

limitations: Uses event data for candidate passes and tracking data for release/reception proof.
Does not infer tactical intention, pass optimality, or decision quality.

definition.description: Maximum ball-player distance for control evidence.
definition.name: control_distance_m

definition.description: Optional provider event-type filter for controlled-pass anchors.
definition.name: event_type_filter

definition.description: Minimum receiver-control dwell for controlled reception.
definition.name: minimum_receiver_dwell_seconds

definition.description: Margin allowing the intended player to count as controlling near teammates.
definition.name: nearest_teammate_margin_m

definition.description: Maximum release-to-controlled-reception search window.
definition.name: reception_search_seconds

definition.description: Search window after the event anchor for ball departure.
definition.name: release_search_after_seconds

definition.description: Search window before the event anchor for passer control.
definition.name: release_search_before_seconds

limitations: Claims only observed ball-target lane screening geometry at the declared frame.
PASS means at least one observed candidate defender lies between ball and target within the frozen lane-distance buffer.
Does not infer defender intent, tactical denial quality, pass probability, pitch-control value, marking assignment, scheme, causation, or optimality.
Missing ball/target tracking, too-short lanes, invalid thresholds, or insufficient observed defender tracking produces UNKNOWN rather than a lane-denial claim.

definition.description: Observed outfield candidate group checked for defender-lane screening geometry.
definition.name: candidate_scope

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field where ball-target lane screening is evaluated.
definition.name: frame_field

definition.description: Maximum perpendicular distance from ball-target lane for cover_shadow_status PASS.
definition.name: maximum_lane_distance_m

definition.description: Minimum ball-target lane length before screening geometry is evaluated.
definition.name: minimum_lane_length_m

definition.description: Minimum observed candidate defenders before PASS/FAIL lane-screening status is claimed.
definition.name: minimum_observed_defenders

definition.description: Minimum normalized along-lane projection distance away from the ball and target endpoints.
definition.name: minimum_projection_fraction

definition.description: Anchor field containing the tracked target entity that defines the lane from the ball.
definition.name: target_entity_field

limitations: Models only an observed geometric defensive line under declared thresholds.
Does not identify a tactical first, second, midfield, or back line.
Does not prove a line break, tactical intention, pass optimality, or defensive intent.

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field used to evaluate the observed line.
definition.name: anchor_frame_field

definition.description: Minimum distance beyond the ball for a defender to count as goal-side.
definition.name: goal_side_buffer_m

definition.description: Maximum normalized x-span of defenders in one observed line band.
definition.name: line_band_width_m

definition.description: Minimum defending outfield players required to form an observed line.
definition.name: minimum_line_defenders

limitations: Goalkeepers are excluded from the outfield centroid.
No pass probability.
No optimality or decision-quality claim.
No receiver body-orientation model.
No offside model in V1 acceptance.

definition.description: Frames required before a corridor is considered closed.
definition.name: close_after_frames

definition.description: Maximum relation search window after the anchor.
definition.name: max_window_seconds

definition.description: Maximum source-target segment length.
definition.name: maximum_segment_length_m

definition.description: Minimum defender clearance from the source-target segment.
definition.name: minimum_clearance_m

definition.description: Minimum open duration retained in the relation output.
definition.name: minimum_duration_seconds

definition.description: Minimum forward progression from source to target.
definition.name: minimum_progression_m

definition.description: Minimum source-target segment length.
definition.name: minimum_segment_length_m

definition.description: Frames required before a corridor is considered open.
definition.name: open_after_frames

definition.description: Retain corridors by relation destination side relative to the ball side.
definition.name: side_filter

limitations: No pass probability.
No optimality or decision-quality claim.
No player intent, causation, or missed-opportunity claim.
No receiver body-orientation or offside model.

definition.description: Frames required before a corridor is considered closed.
definition.name: close_after_frames

definition.description: Maximum relation search window after the anchor.
definition.name: max_window_seconds

definition.description: Maximum source-target segment length.
definition.name: maximum_segment_length_m

definition.description: Minimum defender clearance from the source-target segment.
definition.name: minimum_clearance_m

definition.description: Minimum open duration retained in the relation output.
definition.name: minimum_duration_seconds

definition.description: Minimum forward progression from source to target.
definition.name: minimum_progression_m

definition.description: Minimum source-target segment length.
definition.name: minimum_segment_length_m

definition.description: Frames required before a corridor is considered open.
definition.name: open_after_frames

definition.description: Retain corridors by relation destination side relative to the ball side.
definition.name: side_filter

limitations: Claims only that two evidence rows joined under declared key/status/time/distinctness/same-entity constraints.
Does not infer tactical intent, causation, role semantics, optimality, or decision quality.
Preserves upstream UNKNOWN/FAIL status instead of treating missing evidence as support for a tactical claim.
N-way tactical compositions must be expressed as chains of this binary join.

definition.description: Comma-separated entity-reference fields that must be pairwise distinct after projection.
definition.name: distinct_entity_fields

definition.description: Field read from each left episode to produce the join key.
definition.name: left_key_field

definition.description: Optional status field on the left episode that must equal required_status_value.
definition.name: left_status_field

definition.description: Left frame field used by temporal relations that compare point frames.
definition.name: left_time_field

definition.description: Maximum allowed right_time - left_time gap when temporal_relation checks order.
definition.name: maximum_gap_seconds

definition.description: Status value required when a left or right status field is configured.
definition.name: required_status_value

definition.description: Field read from each right episode to produce the join key.
definition.name: right_key_field

definition.description: Optional status field on the right episode that must equal required_status_value.
definition.name: right_status_field

definition.description: Right frame field used by temporal relations that compare point frames.
definition.name: right_time_field

definition.description: Entity-reference field pair that must be equal after projection.
definition.name: same_entity_fields

definition.description: Optional temporal relation between joined episodes.
definition.name: temporal_relation

limitations: Classifies observed outfield players only; it does not prove complete active-player lane coverage.
Uses fixed lateral lane geometry and does not infer player intention, support quality, optimality, or role semantics.
Does not prove a line break, controlled support arrival, or causal effect.

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field used for lane evaluation.
definition.name: frame_field

definition.description: Observed outfield player group to classify into lanes.
definition.name: player_scope

definition.description: Minimum number of observed lanes that must contain at least one selected player.
definition.name: required_occupied_lane_count

limitations: Observed geometric local count only; does not infer pressure or tactical role.
Does not prove support quality, player intention, causation, optimality, or pass probability.
Uses observed outfield tracking at the declared frame around the anchor reference point.

definition.description: Anchor record frame field where the local-number relation is evaluated.
definition.name: frame_field

definition.description: Maximum observed defending players inside the local region.
definition.name: maximum_defending_players

definition.description: Minimum perspective minus defending local player count.
definition.name: minimum_difference

definition.description: Minimum observed perspective players inside the local region.
definition.name: minimum_perspective_players

definition.description: Radius around the anchor reference point used for observed local counts.
definition.name: radius_m

limitations: Claims only observed nearest-opposition proximity to a target player at the declared frame.
marking_status PASS means the nearest observed candidate is within the frozen distance threshold; unmarked_status PASS means the nearest observed candidate is outside it.
Does not infer marking assignment, defensive scheme, man-or-zone responsibility, role, intent, communication, causation, quality, or optimality.
Missing target tracking or insufficient observed candidate tracking produces UNKNOWN rather than a marked/unmarked claim.

definition.description: Observed outfield candidate group from which the nearest marker is selected.
definition.name: candidate_scope

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field where observed marking proximity is evaluated.
definition.name: frame_field

definition.description: Nearest-opponent distance threshold for marking_status PASS.
definition.name: maximum_marking_distance_m

definition.description: Minimum observed outfield opponents before marked/unmarked can be claimed.
definition.name: minimum_observed_marker_candidates

definition.description: Anchor field containing the target player being checked for observed nearest-opponent proximity.
definition.name: target_player_id_field

limitations: Line ranks are observed geometric ordering only, not tactical first/second/midfield/back-line labels.
Does not prove a line break, tactical intention, pass optimality, causation, or defensive role.

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field used to evaluate observed line bands.
definition.name: anchor_frame_field

definition.description: Minimum distance beyond the ball for a defender to count as goal-side.
definition.name: goal_side_buffer_m

definition.description: Maximum normalized x-span of defenders in one observed line band.
definition.name: line_band_width_m

definition.description: Minimum defending outfield players required to form an observed line band.
definition.name: minimum_line_defenders

definition.description: Geometric goal-side line rank to expose for downstream relative-position tests.
definition.name: target_line_rank

limitations: Claims only observed endpoint-window off-ball movement by a non-ball actor in the declared candidate scope.
PASS requires clear endpoint tracking, displacement, average speed, and distance from the ball under frozen thresholds.
Does not infer run type, run purpose, decoy movement, marker dragging, space creation, role, intent, tactical causation, quality, or optimality.
Directional/path labels are separate derived claims; only explicitly modeled observed path geometry may compose on top of this base movement episode.
Insufficient candidate tracking or missing ball endpoints produce UNKNOWN rather than a fabricated run/no-run claim.

definition.description: Observed outfield candidate group evaluated for off-ball movement.
definition.name: candidate_scope

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field where the run observation window starts.
definition.name: frame_field

definition.description: Forward tracking window used to measure endpoint displacement.
definition.name: lookahead_seconds

definition.description: Maximum endpoint-missing candidate ratio before the run observation is UNKNOWN.
definition.name: maximum_missing_candidate_ratio

definition.description: Minimum distance from the ball at both endpoints for the candidate to count as off-ball.
definition.name: minimum_ball_distance_m

definition.description: Minimum candidate players observed at both endpoints before PASS/FAIL can be claimed.
definition.name: minimum_observed_candidates

definition.description: Minimum endpoint displacement required for off_ball_run_status PASS.
definition.name: minimum_run_displacement_m

definition.description: Minimum endpoint-average speed required for off_ball_run_status PASS.
definition.name: minimum_run_speed_mps

limitations: Classifies only observed path geometry over an already observed off-ball run episode.
run_in_behind_status means the runner moved from not beyond to beyond the observed opposition outfield line under frozen thresholds.
diagonal_run_status means the run had both forward and lateral components under frozen thresholds.
Does not infer decoy movement, marker dragging, space creation, overlap/underlap role, third-player tactical purpose, intent, causation, quality, or optimality.
Insufficient line or tracking coverage produces UNKNOWN for line-relative labels.

definition.description: Minimum forward component for supported forward path labels.
definition.name: minimum_forward_progression_m

definition.description: Minimum lateral component for diagonal_run_status PASS.
definition.name: minimum_lateral_displacement_m

definition.description: Minimum observed opposition outfield defenders at each endpoint before run-in-behind can be evaluated.
definition.name: minimum_observed_defenders

limitations: Requires provider event linkage for v0.1; tracking-only redirection candidates are not modeled.
Claims only observed first-time relay under declared thresholds.
Does not infer tactical intention, third-man combination, pass optimality, or decision quality.

definition.description: Maximum observed relay touch-to-release duration for one-touch status.
definition.name: maximum_relay_dwell_seconds

definition.description: Maximum event-time gap from input pass event to relay pass event.
definition.name: relay_max_event_gap_seconds

definition.description: Maximum ball-player distance for relay-touch evidence.
definition.name: relay_touch_distance_m

limitations: Counts relative ball-position change between release and controlled reception.
Does not prove the pass alone caused every opponent to be bypassed.
Goalkeepers are excluded from the opponent denominator.

definition.description: Reception-frame buffer for deciding whether an opponent is behind the ball.
definition.name: bypassed_buffer_m

definition.description: Release-frame buffer for deciding whether an opponent is goal-side of the ball.
definition.name: goal_side_buffer_m

limitations: Settled possession is a frozen duration threshold, not a judgement of possession quality.
Does not infer intent, counterattack quality, or tactical causation.

definition.description: Maximum time after the anchor in which the settled threshold may be reached.
definition.name: maximum_window_seconds

definition.description: Continuous same-team active-ball possession duration required for SETTLED.
definition.name: minimum_settled_possession_seconds

definition.description: Optional upstream status field that must match before the window is evaluated.
definition.name: required_anchor_status_field

definition.description: Required upstream status value.
definition.name: required_anchor_status_value

limitations: Measures observed geometry only.
Does not infer pressure, marking quality, intent, or optimality.

definition.description: Anchor field containing the first entity ID.
definition.name: entity_a_field

definition.description: Anchor field containing the second entity ID, or ball.
definition.name: entity_b_field

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field where distance is measured.
definition.name: frame_field

definition.description: Threshold used for pairwise_distance_status.
definition.name: maximum_distance_m

limitations: Claims only connected event-linked receipt/relay/terminal controlled reception.
Does not infer planned combination, tactical intent, optimality, or pass probability.
Observed geometric/kinematic pressure only.
Does not infer pressure quality, defensive intent, scanning, decision quality, or causation.
Missing carrier or defender tracking produces UNKNOWN rather than false pressure.

definition.description: Candidate defender scope. v0.1 supports observed defending outfield players.
definition.name: candidate_scope

definition.description: Anchor-record field containing the pressured carrier id.
definition.name: carrier_id_field

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field where pressure is evaluated.
definition.name: frame_field

definition.description: Velocity lookback interval for closing-speed and approach-angle estimates.
definition.name: lookback_seconds

definition.description: Maximum angle between defender velocity and defender-to-carrier vector.
definition.name: maximum_approach_angle_degrees

definition.description: Nearest-defender distance required for observed pressure.
definition.name: maximum_pressure_distance_m

definition.description: Minimum defender-to-carrier closing speed in metres per second.
definition.name: minimum_closing_speed_mps

definition.description: Minimum observed duration satisfying the pressure condition ending at the evaluation frame.
definition.name: minimum_pressure_duration_seconds

limitations: Measures receiver/relay-player position transition, not ball trajectory through a line.
Does not prove a tactical defensive-line break, causation, intention, or decision quality.
Measures entry into relation destination geometry only.
No pass probability, optimality, decision-quality, intent, causation, or missed-opportunity claim.

definition.description: Lookahead horizon for relation destination entry.
definition.name: destination_entry_horizon_seconds

definition.description: Deterministic witness ordering when an anchor has multiple relation episodes.
definition.name: episode_selection

definition.description: Seed used to derive deterministic relation-entry record IDs.
definition.name: result_id_seed

limitations: Classifies only observed position relative to a supplied geometric line.
Does not identify a tactical first, second, midfield, or back line.
Does not prove a line break, tactical intention, pass optimality, or defensive intent.

definition.description: Frame field at which the entity position is evaluated.
definition.name: entity_frame_field

definition.description: Entity-reference field read from the entity anchor record.
definition.name: entity_id_field

definition.description: Distance from the line treated as level rather than ahead or behind.
definition.name: line_buffer_m

limitations: Provider event taxonomy supplies restart/set-piece identity; tracking supplies arrangement evidence.
PASS means a recognized restart event has enough observed outfield players for both teams at the anchor frame.
Does not infer routine variant, planned play, delivery pattern, marking assignment, player role, intent, quality, or tactical causation.

definition.description: Minimum observed outfield players per team required to claim arrangement evidence.
definition.name: minimum_observed_outfield_players

limitations: Claims only sampled open-space candidate geometry from observed outfield-player positions at a declared frame.
PASS means at least the frozen number of sampled pitch points satisfy frozen nearest-player distance thresholds.
Does not infer continuous polygons, pitch control, space value, creation, exploitation, player intent, tactical causation, quality, or optimality.
Invalid direction, invalid grid parameters, or insufficient observed player tracking produces UNKNOWN rather than an open/closed-space claim.

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field where sampled open-space geometry is evaluated.
definition.name: frame_field

definition.description: Pitch-grid spacing for sampled candidate open-space points.
definition.name: grid_step_m

definition.description: Maximum representative candidate points retained as evidence.
definition.name: maximum_candidate_points

definition.description: Minimum observed outfield players for each team before sampled open-space status is claimed.
definition.name: minimum_observed_players_per_team

definition.description: Minimum sampled candidate points required for open_space_status PASS.
definition.name: minimum_open_points

definition.description: Minimum distance from every observed opponent outfielder for a sampled point to count as open.
definition.name: minimum_opponent_distance_m

definition.description: Minimum distance from every observed teammate outfielder for a sampled point to count as open.
definition.name: minimum_teammate_distance_m

definition.description: Optional attacking-direction-normalized region in which candidate points are sampled.
definition.name: zone_scope

limitations: Coarse zones only; not a tactical role or detailed positional-lane taxonomy.
Does not infer team intent, build-up phase quality, or territorial value.

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field where ball-zone membership is evaluated.

definition.description: Boundary buffer that turns near-boundary zone calls into UNKNOWN.

definition.description: Coarse attacking-direction-normalized pitch zone.

limitations: Observed geometric relation only; does not infer tactical intention.
Does not claim support quality, decision quality, communication, scanning, or optimality.
Uses observed outfield tracking around the declared anchor reference point.

definition.description: Anchor record frame field where support timing starts.
definition.name: anchor_frame_field

definition.description: Observed outfield player scope for candidate supporters.

definition.description: Latest arrival after the support anchor frame.

definition.description: Maximum distance from the anchor reference point.

definition.description: Minimum continuous observed duration inside the support region.
definition.name: minimum_duration_seconds

definition.description: Minimum number of observed candidates that must satisfy the support relation.

definition.description: Optional upstream status field that must equal the required value before support is evaluated.

Does not infer a designed switch, role taxonomy, tactical causation, or pass quality.

definition.description: Minimum absolute y-axis displacement from release to reception.
definition.name: minimum_lateral_displacement_m

definition.description: Minimum absolute release y-position for the pass to start wide enough.
definition.name: minimum_start_lateral_m

limitations: Uses observed outfield bounding-box width/depth only.
Does not infer tactical compactness quality, player intent, role assignment, or lane denial.

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field where team compactness is evaluated.
definition.name: frame_field

definition.description: Maximum longitudinal team depth for compactness PASS.
definition.name: maximum_team_depth_m

definition.description: Maximum lateral team width for compactness PASS.
definition.name: maximum_team_width_m

definition.description: Minimum observed outfield players required before compactness can be evaluated.
definition.name: minimum_observed_players

definition.description: Observed outfield team to measure.
definition.name: player_scope

limitations: Claims only observed multi-defender pressure geometry around a carrier at the declared frame.
PASS means multiple observed defenders satisfy frozen distance, closing/approach, and angular-spread thresholds.
Does not infer press trap, pressing trigger, coordinated scheme, defensive communication, intent, causation, pressure quality, or optimality.
Missing carrier tracking, insufficient defender tracking, or missing kinematic evidence produces UNKNOWN rather than a team-press claim.

definition.description: Candidate defender scope. v0.1 supports observed defending outfield players.
definition.name: candidate_scope

definition.description: Anchor-record field containing the pressured carrier id.
definition.name: carrier_id_field

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field where multi-defender pressure is evaluated.
definition.name: frame_field

definition.description: Velocity lookback interval for closing-speed and approach-angle estimates.
definition.name: lookback_seconds

definition.description: Maximum angle between defender velocity and defender-to-carrier vector.
definition.name: maximum_approach_angle_degrees

definition.description: Maximum defender-carrier distance for a defender to count as a team-press candidate.
definition.name: maximum_press_distance_m

definition.description: Minimum angular separation among pressure actors around the carrier.
definition.name: minimum_angle_spread_degrees

definition.description: Minimum defender-to-carrier closing speed for a pressure actor.
definition.name: minimum_closing_speed_mps

definition.description: Minimum observed defending outfield players before PASS/FAIL team-press status is claimed.
definition.name: minimum_observed_defenders

definition.description: Minimum observed defenders satisfying pressure actor criteria.
definition.name: minimum_pressing_defenders

limitations: Evaluates static-point reachability under a declared straight-line speed assumption.
A reachable verdict is an optimistic point-mass bound because current heading and momentum are ignored in v0.1.
Does not infer player intent, effort, pitch control, lane denial quality, or tactical causation.
Does not solve moving-target interception or cover-shadow geometry.
Missing target or insufficient observed candidates produce UNKNOWN.

definition.description: Observed candidate-player group evaluated for arrival.

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field where reachability is evaluated.

definition.description: Threshold used for time_to_arrival_status.

definition.description: Minimum observed candidate-player count required before a PASS/FAIL judgement.
definition.name: minimum_observed_candidates

definition.description: Anchor field containing the target player ID when target_mode=entity.

definition.description: How to resolve the static target point.
definition.name: target_mode

definition.description: Anchor field containing target x when target_mode=fields.
definition.name: target_x_field

definition.description: Anchor field containing target y when target_mode=fields.

limitations: Reports observed coordinate availability only.
Does not infer tracking accuracy beyond missing/usable coordinates in v0.1.

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field where quality is evaluated.

limitations: Uses the observed tracking possession-role stream as the transition source.
Does not infer pressing trigger, transition intent, or tactical phase quality.

definition.description: Perspective-team possession boundary to expose.

definition.description: Boundary buffer that turns near-boundary zone calls into UNKNOWN.

limitations: Simple displacement-derived observed velocity only.
Does not infer effort, intent, role, or pressure quality.
Missing endpoint coordinates produce UNKNOWN.

definition.description: Anchor-record field containing the tracked player id.

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field where velocity is evaluated.

definition.description: Time before the evaluation frame used for displacement velocity.

rejected_sources: generic collection counts not indexed by anchor_id

count_at_least.name: count_at_least
count_at_least.purpose: Count a boolean or entity collection and compare to a count threshold.

eq.name: eq
eq.purpose: Compare a signal to an equal typed value.

rejected_sources: generic collection counts not indexed by anchor_id

exists.name: exists
exists.purpose: Test whether a boolean episode or anchor-evaluation collection exists.

gt.name: gt
gt.purpose: Compare a numeric signal to a strict lower numeric threshold.

gte.name: gte
gte.purpose: Compare a numeric signal to a numeric threshold.

lte.name: lte
lte.purpose: Compare a numeric signal to an upper numeric threshold.

neq.name: neq
neq.purpose: Compare a signal to a non-equal typed value.

limitations: UNKNOWN remains UNKNOWN; absence is not converted to FALSE.
persists_for.name: persists_for
persists_for.purpose: Promote a boolean frame signal to intervals after minimum duration.

possession_corridor_destination_entry.claims_boundary: Measures geometric ball entry into a relation destination region only; no pass probability, optimality, intent, causation, or missed-opportunity claim.
possession_corridor_destination_entry.purpose: Agent-authorable generic composition for possession-start progressive corridors followed by ball entry into the relation destination region.

limitations: No pass probability.
No optimality or decision-quality claim.
No player intent, causation, or missed-opportunity claim.
No receiver body-orientation or offside model.

definition.description: Frames required before a corridor is considered closed.
definition.name: close_after_frames

definition.description: Maximum relation search window after the anchor.
definition.name: max_window_seconds

definition.description: Maximum source-target segment length.
definition.name: maximum_segment_length_m

definition.description: Minimum defender clearance from the source-target segment.
definition.name: minimum_clearance_m

definition.description: Minimum open duration retained in the relation output.
definition.name: minimum_duration_seconds

definition.description: Minimum forward progression from source to target.
definition.name: minimum_progression_m

definition.description: Minimum source-target segment length.
definition.name: minimum_segment_length_m

definition.description: Frames required before a corridor is considered open.
definition.name: open_after_frames

definition.description: Retain corridors by relation destination side relative to the ball side.
definition.name: side_filter

limitations: Measures entry into relation destination geometry only.
No pass probability, optimality, decision-quality, intent, causation, or missed-opportunity claim.

definition.description: Lookahead horizon for relation destination entry.
definition.name: destination_entry_horizon_seconds

definition.description: Deterministic witness ordering when an anchor has multiple relation episodes.
definition.name: episode_selection

definition.description: Seed used to derive deterministic relation-entry record IDs.
definition.name: result_id_seed

eq.name: eq
eq.purpose: Compare a signal to an equal typed value.

rejected_sources: generic collection counts not indexed by anchor_id

exists.purpose: Test whether a boolean episode or anchor-evaluation collection exists.

required_safety_rules: Use relation_destination_entry.entry_status with eq PASS for destination-region entry.
Do not invent operators or catalog refs.
Do not use relation_destination_entry_classification in agent-authored plans.

limitations: No pass probability.
No optimality or decision-quality claim.
No player intent, causation, or missed-opportunity claim.
No receiver body-orientation or offside model.

eq.name: eq
eq.purpose: Compare a signal to an equal typed value.

rejected_sources: generic collection counts not indexed by anchor_id
